request_debug.prompt_preview:
You write per-candidate post-mortems for a slopmortem pipeline. Given a fresh pitch and one dead-startup canonical document, produce a single LLMSynthesis JSON object that explains why this dead startup is similar, where the new pitch could diverge, what killed the dead startup, and what the new founder should learn.

Rules:
- Ground every claim in the candidate document or in tool calls. Do not invent facts.
- A `Trusted facts` block (typed pipeline data — not user input) appears below the pitc

response.text: {"candidate_id":"rethinkdb::devtools","name":"rethinkdb","one_liner":"Free, open-source distributed document database with real-time change feeds, shut down in 2016 after failing to build a sustainable commercial business around an open-source core.","similarity":{"business_model":{"score":3,"rationale":"The new pitch is a paid B2B SaaS with a hosted control plane — a clear subscription or usage-based revenue model. RethinkDB was free and open-source with no meaningful commercial monetization layer; Trusted facts list price_point as free and monetization as other. The two models share a developer-first orientation but diverge sharply on commercialization strategy."},"market":{"score":4,"rationale":"Both target developers (confirmed by Trusted facts customer_type: developer) globally (geography: global). However, RethinkDB served the distributed database sub-sector, while the new pitch targets the cloud infrastructure / developer-tooling sub-sector for preview environments. The buyer persona and community overlap, but the addressable problem space is different."},"gtm":{"score":5,"rationale":"Both rely on developer adoption as the primary GTM motion — self-serve, community-driven, bottoms-up. RethinkDB used open-source distribution to drive awareness; the new pitch uses a CLI (a classic PLG on-ramp). The parallel is meaningful even though the new pitch also layers on a hosted control plane to capture commercial value, which RethinkDB never succeeded at."},"stage_scale":{"score":4,"rationale":"RethinkDB was seed-funded via YC in 2009 and ran for roughly 7 years before shutting down, suggesting early-to-growth stage throughout. The new pitch appears to be at an early/seed stage. Both are pre-scale, developer-infrastructure plays, but RethinkDB's extended runway makes a direct stage comparison only partial."}},"why_similar":"Both products target developers globally with a self-serve, bottoms-up GTM anchored on a CLI or developer-native tooling. Both operate in infrastructure adjacent to cloud-native workloads. RethinkDB's core lesson — that developer love and open-source adoption do not automatically translate to revenue — is directly applicable to any developer-infrastructure startup, including one selling a hosted control plane around a free or open entry point.","where_diverged":"The most concrete divergence is monetization model: the new pitch explicitly bundles a hosted, paid control plane (B2B SaaS) alongside the CLI, creating a clear commercial upsell path from day one. RethinkDB was free and open-source with no successful commercial tier (Trusted facts: price_point = free, monetization = other). Additionally, the new pitch targets a different sub-sector (ephemeral preview environments / cloud DevOps tooling) rather than distributed databases, and the product architecture — stateless, ephemeral environments vs. a stateful database engine — implies a fundamentally different operational and cost structure.","failure_causes":["inability to monetize open-source user base","no viable commercial tier or enterprise offering","free price point with no upsell path","developer adoption without revenue conversion","engineering-focused culture lacking commercial go-to-market","market timing mismatch (real-time DB features ahead of mainstream demand)","loss of engineering team to acqui-hire (Stripe)"],"lessons_for_input":["Define and gate the paid hosted control plane tier before launch — do not let the free CLI become the entire product in users' minds.","Establish a clear conversion funnel from CLI usage to paid control-plane seats with measurable activation milestones.","Avoid over-indexing on developer community metrics (GitHub stars, downloads) as proxies for business health; track paying customers and expansion revenue from day one.","Retain your founding engineering team by building commercial traction early — acqui-hire risk rises sharply once the market perceives the product as stalled.","Validate willingness-to-pay in the enterprise or mid-market segment before scaling open-source distribution, so community growth funds rather than replaces revenue growth."]}
